title: Scheduled Monitoring, Digests, and Public Intelligence Feeds
principles: Monitoring checks evaluate public source records and do not prove that no real-world event occurred when no match is found.
Duplicate alerts are suppressed by transparent fingerprints and configurable time windows.
Daily and weekly digests remain drafts until a human explicitly approves or publishes them.
Public RSS, Atom, and JSON feeds do not require hosted subscriber profiles or tracking.
Email and webhook delivery adapters are optional and disabled by default.
Quiet periods, notification limits, source failures, and stale-data conditions remain visible.
